channel.title: CyberNetSec - Cloud Security
channel.description: Latest cloud security threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: HollowGraph Malware Uses Microsoft 365 Calendar for Covert C2
item.description:
A sophisticated espionage implant named HollowGraph is using Microsoft 365 calendar events for command-and-control (C2) and data exfiltration. The malware, discovered by Group-IB, hijacks a compromised user's calendar and hides operator instructions and stolen data within events dated to the year 20...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Cyberattack, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Data-Wiping Attacks on the Rise, Abusing Legitimate Admin Tools
item.description:
The supply chain industry is being warned about a rising trend of destructive data-wiping attacks. A key example is the March 2026 incident at medical tech firm Stryker, where the Iranian hacktivist group Handala Hack Team claimed to have used Microsoft Intune to issue remote wipe commands to 80,000...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Actor, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Report: Enterprises Overwhelmed by Disjointed Security Tools Amid AI Risks
item.description:
A report from Thales reveals that enterprises are using a multitude of separate security tools, leading to policy inconsistencies and visibility gaps. This 'tool sprawl,' where organizations use an average of seven different tools for data protection alone, is creating a significant and expanding at...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ernst & Young Discloses Data Breach via Third-Party Support System
item.description:
Global professional services firm Ernst & Young (EY) has disclosed a data breach that occurred via a compromised third-party IT service management platform. Between March and April 2026, an unauthorized actor gained access to the system and downloaded documents containing sensitive personal and fina...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Supply Chain Attack, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: 23andMe Reaches $18M Bankruptcy Settlement Over 2023 Data Breach
item.description:
Genetic testing company 23andMe has agreed to an $18 million bankruptcy settlement with 43 U.S. states, including Pennsylvania, over a massive data breach discovered in October 2023. The breach, which impacted nearly 7 million customers, was attributed to credential stuffing attacks that succeeded d...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Regulatory, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: New security tools target deepfakes, risky OAuth, and AI agent threats
item.description:
This week saw the launch of several new security products aimed at addressing emerging enterprise threats. Polygraf AI introduced 'Meeting Guard' for real-time deepfake detection in video meetings. Nudge Security released capabilities to automate the discovery and remediation of risky OAuth grants a...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Unpatched Flaws in Claude Chrome Extension Expose Google Workspace Data
item.description:
Two unpatched vulnerabilities in Anthropic's 'Claude for Chrome' extension could allow a second, malicious extension to access and exfiltrate a user's Google Workspace data, including Gmail, Docs, and Calendar. The flaws, which exploit the extension's trust model by allowing a script to trigger acti...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Vulnerability, Cloud Security, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: AsyncAPI Supply Chain Attack on NPM Deploys Miasma RAT
item.description:
A sophisticated supply chain attack has compromised several official AsyncAPI npm packages. Attackers gained push access to the project's GitHub 'next' branch, using the legitimate CI/CD pipeline to publish malicious versions containing the Miasma RAT. This technique bypassed typical defenses by usi...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Canada Warns Banks of AI Risks from Anthropic's Claude Mythos
item.description:
Canada's financial regulator, the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI), has issued a warning to the country's major banks and insurers about the cybersecurity threats posed by advanced AI models, specifically citing Anthropic's Claude Mythos. In an April 2026 confidential al...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Cloud Security, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Accenture Downplays Breach as Hacker '888' Claims Theft of Source Code and Cloud Keys
item.description:
A threat actor named '888' has claimed to have stolen 35GB of highly sensitive data from consulting giant Accenture, including source code, cryptographic keys, and Azure access tokens. The hacker posted evidence on a cybercrime forum, offering the data for sale. Accenture has acknowledged an 'isolat...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →
